send_reply.label: Send Reply
send_reply.description: Send a visible reply to the current group chat thread.
send_reply.promptSnippet: Send a visible reply to the current group chat thread
promptGuidelines: Use send_reply whenever you need to make a visible group-chat reply.
Final assistant text is hidden in group chats; do not rely on it for visible answers.
If a group-chat transcript does not need a visible bot response, do not call send_reply.

text.description: Visible message text to send to the group chat.

send_attachment.label: Send Attachment
send_attachment.description: Send a local file to the current chat. Relative paths resolve from the current workdir.
send_attachment.promptSnippet: Send a local file attachment to the current chat
promptGuidelines: Use send_attachment to send generated local files to the chat.
groupPromptGuidelines: Use send_reply in group chats for explanatory text that should accompany an attachment.

path.description: Local file path. Relative paths resolve from the current workdir.

add_schedule.label: Add Schedule
add_schedule.description: Add a heartbeat or background schedule for the current chat. Heartbeat wakes the front agent directly; background runs isolated work first and routes its result back to the front agent.
add_schedule.promptSnippet: Add a recurring or one-time scheduled task for the current chat
promptGuidelines: Use add_schedule when the user asks for future, recurring, delayed, reminder, polling, monitoring, or self-wakeup work, even if the wording is informal.
Choose heartbeat for lightweight chat-contextual work where the front agent should act directly when the schedule fires.
Choose background for heavier inspection, research, repo scanning, trigger detection, or independent operation that should not occupy the current chat context.
Background tasks may detect whether action is needed, perform safe independent work, or both; their final result will be passed back to the front agent before any visible chat reply.
Use trigger=cron with cron for recurring schedules; cron is minute-level only.
Use trigger=once with run_at for one-time schedules; run_at is second-level.
If the user does not specify a timezone for a one-time schedule, use the runtime local timezone: {{local_timezone}} (UTC{{local_utc_offset}}).
Put only the future task instruction in task. Make it outcome-focused and self-contained.

Workflow-backed LOOP shapes include content pipelines (explore -> create -> edit -> publish/report), research cycles (collect -> analyze -> critique -> synthesize), event planning (requirements -> logistics -> budget/risk -> action list), experiments (prepare -> run -> measure -> report), and implementation pipelines (check -> implement -> review -> fix/synthesize).

For workflow-backed LOOP tasks, reference an existing saved workflow by name or path; do not put workflow JavaScript in task, and do not include fallback instructions that bypass the workflow if it is unavailable — record a blocker instead.
To replace an existing schedule, remove it first, then add the new schedule.
Do not put scheduling instructions, schedule names, or requests to create/list/remove schedules inside task.
groupPromptGuidelines: After add_schedule in group chat, call send_reply to confirm the schedule if a visible confirmation is needed.

mode.description: heartbeat wakes the front agent directly with chat context and Pievo tools; use it for lightweight reminders/checks and user-visible follow-up. background runs an isolated agent first without chat/schedule tools, then passes its final result back to the front agent; use it for heavy checks, trigger detection, repo/research scans, or independent work.

name.description: Schedule name: letters, numbers, underscore, or dash.

trigger.description: Schedule trigger type. Use cron for recurring schedules and once for one-time schedules. Defaults from whether cron or run_at is provided.

cron.description: Five-field cron expression for recurring schedules: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week. Minute-level only. Required when trigger is cron; omit when trigger is once.

run_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for a one-time schedule with seconds and timezone offset, for example 2026-06-22T09:00:00{{local_utc_offset}}. Required when trigger is once; omit when trigger is cron. If the user did not specify a timezone, use the runtime local timezone: {{local_timezone}} (UTC{{local_utc_offset}}).

skip_if_active.description: Whether to skip a firing when the same schedule is already active. Defaults to true for heartbeat and background schedules; set false only when the user explicitly wants overlapping runs.

task.description: Task instruction to execute when the schedule fires. Describe the desired future outcome and enough context for that future run; exclude cadence, schedule setup/removal/listing instructions, phrases like "create a schedule", and inline workflow scripts. Workflow-backed tasks should reference an existing saved workflow by name or path.

list_schedule.label: List Schedules
list_schedule.description: List schedules for the current chat.
list_schedule.promptSnippet: List scheduled tasks for the current chat
promptGuidelines: Use list_schedule when the user asks what scheduled tasks exist for this chat.
groupPromptGuidelines: After list_schedule in group chat, call send_reply with the list if a visible answer is needed.

remove_schedule.label: Remove Schedule
remove_schedule.description: Remove a schedule from the current chat by name.
remove_schedule.promptSnippet: Remove a scheduled task from the current chat
promptGuidelines: Use remove_schedule only when the user explicitly asks to remove a scheduled task.
groupPromptGuidelines: After remove_schedule in group chat, call send_reply to confirm removal if a visible confirmation is needed.
